title: spergularia marina var. tenuis
intro: Spergularia marina var. tenuis, also known as sea sandwort, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been used in folk medicine to treat conditions such as arthritis, gout, and skin irritations.
